meaning: adj.粗俗的,庸俗的,普通的,通俗的,本土的n.<古>平民,百姓
examples: I think it's a very vulgar house.
I think that is the most vulgar and tasteless remark I have ever heard in my life.
The speaker played to the gallery by indulging in vulgar jokes.
If anything, his decision to re-tweet a series of vulgar comments rallied fans to his defense.
Mr. RAYMOND: You know, but, I mean, I don't plan on every doing anything too vulgar.
